claims.allowed_statement: Public benchmark claims are authored statements with explicit boundaries.
forbidden_extensions: Raw artifacts automatically generate public claims.
A public claim is valid merely because many artifacts exist.
A boundary table proves the underlying benchmark result.
claims.allowed_replacement: Raw artifacts may support an authored public claim, but they do not automatically define its public wording.

claims.allowed_statement: Each allowed claim should name the stronger statements it does not permit.
forbidden_extensions: A narrow claim can safely imply broader conclusions.
Unstated limits are enough to prevent overclaim.
Readers can infer all boundaries from context.
claims.allowed_replacement: A claim boundary should explicitly state its forbidden expansions.

claims.allowed_statement: A forbidden claim should have a safer replacement that remains publishable.
forbidden_extensions: Every rejected phrase should simply be deleted.
Bounded publication language is necessarily uninformative.
claims.allowed_replacement: Replace an overstrong phrase with a narrower statement that the boundary permits.

claims.allowed_statement: This public method table demonstrates claim-boundary structure without publishing benchmark findings.
forbidden_extensions: The table discloses hidden run evidence.
claims.allowed_replacement: The table is a method sketch showing how public claims can be bounded.

non_claims: This table does not publish benchmark findings.
This table does not prove benchmark truth.
This table does not prove scorer correctness.
This table does not claim general model behavior.
This table does not claim that any undisclosed validation check is complete.
This table does not claim any universal artifact-content guarantee.
